opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — my son caught a hard, rough bump on his finger while he was playing and now it won't stop bleeding. It's bleeding way more than a normal cut and he's really upset.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He snagged the bump on a door frame about an hour ago and part of it tore. We've been holding a tissue on it but it keeps bleeding and we can't get it to stop.

symptom_narrative.onset: the bleeding started about an hour ago when he caught the bump on a door frame while playing; the rough bumps themselves have been there for about 6 months
symptom_narrative.duration: about an hour of on-and-off bleeding from the torn bump; the bumps have been present for roughly half a year
symptom_narrative.character: the bump is hard, rough and fleshy-greyish; the top of the biggest one has partly torn off and the raw base underneath is oozing bright blood, more than we'd expect from a small graze
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it looks like a lot of blood for such a small spot and it soaks through the tissue, but he's alert and not pale or floppy — mostly he's frightened and sore at the spot
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the back of his right index finger, on the biggest of several rough bumps; there are a few smaller ones on the next finger too
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it slows a bit when we press but starts up again whenever we lift the tissue to look
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing on it seems to slow it down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lifting the tissue off to check, and when he knocks or moves the finger
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, he doesn't seem unwell in himself, and the finger isn't hot, puffy or full of pus — it's just the bleeding and the fright
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the rough bumps have been there for months and never hurt or bled before today; he sometimes scratches or picks at them and a few new little ones have appeared
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he's tearful and holding the finger wrapped in a bloody tissue, but he's otherwise up and about

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no ongoing medical problems; no known bleeding or bruising tendency

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: none — he doesn't take any regular medicines

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: otherwise healthy primary-school-aged child, lives at home with his parents; a parent is with him and made the call

objective_data_offered.value: on the back of the right index finger there's a small hard, rough, cauliflower-textured bump about the size of a pea; the top layer has torn and the raw base underneath is oozing bright red blood, and there are tiny dark specks in it; a few smaller similar rough bumps sit on the next finger
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described and shown by the parent over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: an alert, slightly tearful child sitting up and holding the finger wrapped in a blood-stained tissue; not pale, sweaty, floppy or drowsy
the tissue is blood-stained and the spot re-bleeds each time it is unwrapped; it slows when pressure is held; no spurting or pooling of large amounts of blood reported
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent estimate of blood loss only; no measured volume

objective_data_offered.value: we haven't taken his temperature but he doesn't feel hot and hasn't been unwell
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent impression only; no thermometer reading taken

objective_data_offered.value: the finger around the bump isn't red, hot, puffy or leaking pus, and there are no red streaks running up his hand or arm that we can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent self-inspection only; cannot exclude early infection by examination
